title: Cochlearia sphaerocarpa
intro: Cochlearia sphaerocarpa, also known as Sphaero-carrot or Globe Whitlowgrass, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Brassicaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Cochlearia sphaerocarpa include glucosinolates, saponins, and volatile oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal medicine, and its preparations include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, boost the immune system, and exhibit antimicrobial activity.
taxonomy: Cochlearia sphaerocarpa is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. It is native to the Iberian Peninsula and is a member of the genus Cochlearia, which includes approximately 40 species of annual or perennial plants. The genus Cochlearia is further classified under the order Brassicales, which is a large order of flowering plants. The order Brassicales is part of the clade Rosidae, a group of flowering plants that includes many families with showy flowers. As a member of the clade Rosidae, Cochlearia sphaerocarpa is a part of the larger group of flowering plants known as the eudicots.
uses: Cochlearia sphaerocarpa has been traditionally used in folk medicine, though more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may be beneficial in treating various ailments. Some studies suggest that extracts from this plant may have antioxidant effects and potential applications in treating skin conditions or reducing oxidative stress. However, the plant's medicinal uses are not well-documented, and further research is necessary to understand its full potential. More studies are required to validate its traditional uses and confirm its safety for human consumption.

preparations: Cochlearia sphaerocarpa is used in herbal preparations due to its potential medicinal properties.
